landing.title: mobench
landing.description: Benchmark intense Rust workloads on real Android and iOS devices with CLI, SDK, BrowserStack CI, reports, and profiling.

docsRoot.title: mobench docs
docsRoot.description: Architecture, install paths, CLI man pages, SDK integration, BrowserStack App Automate, profiling, schemas, and examples.

docsPages.label: Overview
docsPages.description: mobench is a Rust mobile benchmarking toolkit for Android and iOS, with a CLI, SDK runtime, macro-based benchmarks, generated mobile runners, CI reports, and profiling artifacts.

docsPages.label: Quickstart
docsPages.description: Install the CLI, register a benchmark, build mobile runners, and run locally, on emulators or simulators, or on BrowserStack.

docsPages.label: Installation
docsPages.description: Set up the Rust workspace, mobile toolchains, BrowserStack credentials when needed, and the project configuration file.

docsPages.label: Concepts
docsPages.description: Understand benchmark definitions, generated runners, local and hosted providers, reports, schemas, and profiling output.

docsPages.label: Writing benchmarks
docsPages.description: Use the benchmark macro, setup inputs, teardown functions, and registry discovery rules to expose Rust functions to mobench.

docsPages.label: Setup & teardown
docsPages.description: Keep expensive initialization and cleanup outside measured samples while still giving every iteration the input shape it needs.

docsPages.label: SDK integration
docsPages.description: Add mobench-sdk to a crate, configure runner backends, export native C ABI when needed, and use public runtime types.

docsPages.label: Build artifacts
docsPages.description: Generate Android and iOS runner projects, understand runner backends, and verify outputs under target/mobench.

docsPages.label: Local devices
docsPages.description: Run against connected phones, Android emulators via Android Studio and ADB, and iOS simulators or connected devices via Xcode.

docsPages.label: BrowserStack CI
docsPages.description: Use hosted real devices, matrix resolution, split-sample merging, PR reporting, artifact fetching, and normalized resource metrics.

docsPages.label: App Automate setup
docsPages.description: Set up BrowserStack App Automate for mobench Android Espresso and iOS XCUITest runs, including API uploads, build execution, devices, trial limits, and pricing expectations.

docsPages.label: Profiling
docsPages.description: Run local native profiling, produce trace events, semantic phase summaries, flamegraphs, and profile diffs.

docsPages.label: Outputs & reports
docsPages.description: Understand summary JSON, Markdown summaries, CSV rows, split-run outputs, BrowserStack artifacts, PR comments, and profiling bundles.

docsPages.label: CLI man pages
docsPages.description: Exhaustive command reference for mobench: commands, subcommands, flags, expected inputs, outputs, defaults, and common examples.

docsPages.label: Schemas
docsPages.description: Machine-readable contracts for CI metadata, benchmark summaries, trace events, and fixture payloads.

docsPages.label: Examples explained
docsPages.description: What the basic benchmark and native C ABI / FFI examples demonstrate, and how their fixture outputs map to reports.

docsPages.label: Diagrams
docsPages.description: Architecture, benchmark lifecycle, BrowserStack CI, profiling artifacts, and SDK versus CLI responsibility diagrams.

docsPages.label: Current behavior
docsPages.description: Current behavior and API contract for release 0.1.46: CLI, config, runners, public APIs, schemas, and compatibility boundaries.

docsPages.label: Codebase architecture
docsPages.description: Workspace layout, runtime layers, generated templates, integrations, stack, and ownership boundaries.

docsPages.label: Testing strategy
docsPages.description: Host tests, CLI smoke checks, split-run merge validation, CI contract checks, BrowserStack smoke tests, and profiling checks.

docsPages.label: Public API
docsPages.description: Published crates, feature flags, serialized contracts, error boundaries, semver policy, MSRV, and release readiness.

docsPages.label: Troubleshooting
docsPages.description: Common setup, build, discovery, BrowserStack, profiling, schema, and result-quality issues.
